goal: 补全 PBT 测试：引入 rapid 框架，覆盖 CanonicalHash/JSON 关系、sync/reconcile 幂等、replay 一致性、state machine 完备性、revert 的 note 表现、多 actor 并发、schema 兼容、thread append-only，并修复两个名实不符的测试

summary.title: Expand PBT coverage with rapid; fix two name/behaviour mismatches and a sync ordering bug
summary.what: Introduced pgregory.net/rapid as the project's PBT framework. Added internal/core/property_test.go (CanonicalHash==sha256(CanonicalJSON), JSON-roundtrip stability, exhaustive state-machine matrix, reachability) and internal/engine/property_test.go (sync idempotency, reconcile idempotency by stripping and re-attaching merge notes, replay consistency, revert-note shape, concurrent ID-generator uniqueness, forward-compatible CommitNote schema, append-only Turn log). Renamed TestPropertyCannotAbandonMerged into TestSealedLocalCanBeAbandoned and added a real merged→abandoned rejection test. Replaced TestPropertyCommitNoteCanonicalHash with one that actually calls core.CanonicalHash and asserts mutability. Promoted gitCmd/writeFile/testRepo to take a helperTB interface so *rapid.T (a TB implementation) can drive them. Patched sync.scanMainNotes to replay main-branch notes chronologically — previously LogOneline returned newest-first and a revert commit on top of a merge commit was masked by the later-applied merge state. Updated the Makefile so `make test` runs with -short (rapid drops to 20 samples per property) and added a new `test-pbt` target plus made `ci` use it for the full 100-sample run.
summary.why: The original PBT suite was hand-rolled with math/rand loops, had no shrinking, hardcoded iteration counts, and shipped two tests whose names contradicted what they actually checked. Several invariants the engine relies on — sync/reconcile being safe to call repeatedly, the view being a reproducible projection of the actor log + notes, the revert path showing through scanMainNotes, the CommitNote schema staying readable across versions — were entirely unverified. A rapid-based suite that exercises those invariants caught a real ordering bug in scanMainNotes that the existing tests had missed.
summary.user_goal: 补全 PBT 测试，覆盖之前缺漏的所有 properties，并修复发现的实现 bug

decisions.rationale: Native Go, supports shrinking, integrates with *testing.T via the TB interface, last release Aug 2024 — most-used Go PBT library.

decisions.chose: Promote testRepo/gitCmd/writeFile to a small helperTB interface (Helper, Fatal, Fatalf)
decisions.rationale: *rapid.T already implements rapid.TB which is a superset of the helpers' needs, so no adapter type is required and integration tests still work unchanged.
rejected: Wrap *rapid.T in a stub struct (extra plumbing for no benefit since rapid.T already exposes the methods)
Duplicate the helpers inside property_test.go (DRY violation, drift risk)

decisions.chose: Strip merge notes after seedMergedIntent so reconcile actually has work on the first call
decisions.rationale: Without this, view.Status would already be merged and reconcile would be a no-op on the very first call; the test would then prove nothing about the catch-up path.

decisions.chose: make test runs -short (20 samples each); add make test-pbt for the full 100
decisions.rationale: Full PBT pass takes ~7m; -short keeps make test under ~2m for the inner loop while CI still gets full coverage via the new test-pbt target.

decisions.chose: Reverse entries to chronological order before applying notes
decisions.rationale: Matches how ReadActorLogEvents already reverses to chronological. Single-pass replay then naturally lets a later revert overwrite an earlier merge state for the same intent.
rejected: Two-pass scan (first intents, then reverts) — works but encodes a hidden ordering rule into the data model

risks: make test now adds ~2m to the inner loop because of the rapid PBTs — still acceptable but could grow as more properties are added.

The chronological replay in scanMainNotes assumes the oldest-applicable note wins for ambiguous cases (only the same intent appearing multiple times); if future workflows write multiple competing notes the precedence rule may need an explicit policy.
followups: Consider letting `mainline init` commit .gitignore so future `git add .` from agents won't accidentally pick up .ml-cache contents.
Add a `Reconcile` PBT that asserts NotesAdd's existing -f overwrite cannot strip an unrelated intent already attached to the same commit.
Wire the rapid `-rapid.checks=N` and `-rapid.failfile` flags into the Makefile help text once the suite stabilises.

architectural_claims: PBTs in this repo are written against pgregory.net/rapid; new properties should follow that pattern.
Helpers in internal/engine that previously took *testing.T now take helperTB so PBT callbacks can drive them.
behavioral_changes: sync.scanMainNotes now replays commit notes oldest-first; revert commits applied after merge commits correctly leave the intent in `reverted` state.
make test runs in -short mode by default; full PBT coverage is available via make test-pbt (and runs in CI).

api_changes.signature: engine.testRepo / gitCmd / writeFile now accept a helperTB interface instead of *testing.T
